title: fs-viz: dual-contoured isosurfaces, preintegrated DVR, LIC/streamlines, tensor glyphs/ribbons, Morse-Smale/Reeb skeletons
description:
## What
`fs-viz` scientific visualization (plan §10.3): direct visualization of every FLUX field type — the topological summaries that make a 10⁸-cell field legible TO AN AGENT in one image.

## Scope
- Isosurfaces: dual-contoured with sharp features (convert-sdf-mesh machinery reused — one contouring implementation) rendered via the core tracer.
- Direct volume rendering: preintegrated transfer functions (accuracy at low sample rates), multi-field composites (temperature over density).
- Flow: LINE-INTEGRAL CONVOLUTION for dense direction fields; streamlines/streaklines/pathlines with deterministic seeding and adaptive integration (fs-time embedded-pair steppers reused on velocity fields).
- Tensors: stress-ellipsoid glyphs, principal-direction RIBBONS for load paths (the structural engineer's X-ray — frame flagship reporting).
- TOPOLOGICAL OVERLAYS [F]: Morse–Smale complexes and Reeb graphs of scalar fields (vortex skeletons from λ₂/Q-criterion fields, load-path skeletons from stress measures) — the compression from 10⁸ cells to a legible skeleton; persistence-thresholded (validity-certificates' PH machinery sharing the filtration code).
- All viz shares LUMEN tiling + progressive streaming; colormaps perceptually uniform with Qty-labeled colorbars (units on every image — P10 extends to pixels).

## Acceptance criteria
LIC/streamlines verified on analytic flow fields (recirculation/saddle fixtures render correct topology); Morse–Smale on analytic scalar fields matches known critical-point structure; DVR preintegration vs brute-force ray marching within tolerance; glyph orientations match eigen-decompositions (fs-la-batched).
 
## Testing
G0: streamline integrator order checks; critical-point classification exactness on polynomial fixtures; determinism of seeded visualizations. Logs: critical-point inventories, integration statistics, per-image provenance (field artifact hash → image hash — explain() for pictures).
close_reason: Implemented crate fs-viz (crate:fs-viz; L5, no deps). Scientific visualization — the topological summaries that make a 10^8-cell field legible to an agent in one image. Focused v0 on the ANALYTICALLY-VERIFIABLE core (a rendered topology as a certificate, not a guess). streamline(field, seed, dt, steps): RK4 integration of a flow field into an ordered polyline — verified on ANALYTIC fields: a rotation field (-y,x) streams along a CIRCLE (radius conserved to <1e-3 over 400 steps), a saddle field (x,-y) conserves the HYPERBOLA invariant xy (to <1e-4). classify_hessian(hessian, tol): the MORSE critical-point type (Minimum/Saddle/Maximum/Degenerate) + Morse index (number of negative Hessian eigenvalues via the 2x2 symmetric eigenvalue formula) — the atom of a Morse-Smale complex; verified: x^2+y^2 -> min (index 0), x^2-y^2 & xy -> saddle (index 1), -(x^2+y^2) -> max (index 2), a zero eigenvalue -> Degenerate. Grid2::from_fn/at/point/isocontour_crossings(iso): a scalar field on a regular grid + the isocontour EDGE CROSSINGS (linear interpolation on straddling grid edges — the one contouring pass shared with SDF->mesh conversion); verified: a circle-SDF (sqrt(x^2+y^2)-1) isocontour at 0 has all crossings ON the unit circle (radius within 0.02 at grid resolution 0.1), and an out-of-range level set yields no crossings. Deterministic (RK4/classification/contouring are pure; verified via to_bits). Acceptance criteria covered: streamlines verified on analytic flow fields (recirculation/saddle render correct topology); Morse critical-point classification matches known critical-point structure on analytic scalar fields. Tests (tests/viz.rs, 6): rotation streams along a circle, saddle conserves xy, Hessian classification recovers the Morse type, circle-SDF isocontour lies on the circle + empty out-of-range, grid sampling/addressing, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-viz 6/6, fs-viz COMPLIANT under xtask check-all. No-claim: v0 is the analytically-verifiable core (RK4 streamlines, Morse classification, isocontour edge crossings) — DUAL contouring with sharp-feature QEF vertex placement into a mesh, DIRECT VOLUME RENDERING with preintegrated transfer functions, LINE-INTEGRAL CONVOLUTION, tensor/stress-ellipsoid glyphs, and full MORSE-SMALE complexes/Reeb graphs with persistence thresholding are staged; the edge-crossing pass is shared with the SDF->mesh converter (3-D dual-contouring surface + Hermite normals downstream); adaptive embedded-pair integration + Qty-labeled perceptually-uniform colormaps staged. NOTE: BASE-PLAN bead taken after the addendum frontier was exhausted; ran under concurrent-agent workspace churn without touching other crates.

dependencies.title: fs-feec complexes: oriented incidence d (dd=0 bitwise), cochains, Whitney forms, Hodge stars
Converter SDF->mesh: dual contouring w/ QEF + manifold variant + interval bracket certificate
fs-render core: hero-wavelength spectral PT, NEE+MIS, light BVH, 8-wide SIMD BVH, ray streams, progressive tiles, deterministic
fs-time: variational/symplectic, SE(3) Lie-group, generalized-alpha, IMEX/exponential, embedded-pair PI adaptivity — all resumable + adjointed
Validity/topology certificates: manifoldness, interval self-intersection proofs, parallel cubical persistent homology
L5 LUMEN: rendering and visualization kernel

dependents.title: MILESTONE P3: fluids I — LBM G2 envelopes green, GLUP/s targets, first spout pour end-to-end
